region_of_origin: Singapore. Manufactured by Fraser & Neave (F&N) Ltd, Singapore. Heaven and Earth is one of Singapore's leading ready-to-drink tea brands, sold widely in convenience stores, supermarkets, and hawker centres across Singapore and the region.
food_subgroup: Ready-to-drink sweetened jasmine-infused green tea. 1 bottle = 500ml. Ingredients: water, sugar, green tea extract, jasmine flavouring, vitamin C (as antioxidant). Ultra-processed commercial beverage.

ontology.foodon_label: green tea beverage (closest parent: tea beverage)
ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn covers tea beverages generally (FOODON_03301103 tea beverage). No specific term exists for RTD sweetened jasmine green tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tea and tea-based beverages (FoodEx2 Level 1 group: Non-alcoholic beverages; subgroup: Tea). Closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for RTD sweetened green tea.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for RTD sweetened jasmine green tea. AN000A (tea and tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for RTD sweetened jasmine green tea. Not clinically coded in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is 1 bottle = 500ml (approximately 500g), providing 85 kcal and 20g sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Heaven and Earth Jasmine Green Tea — Nutrition Information Panel, Fraser & Neave Singapore 500ml bottle product label
nutrient_source.organization: Fraser & Neave Ltd (F&N) Singapore — Singapore Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) per regulatory requirement under Singapore Food Regulations
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) printed on the Heaven and Earth Jasmine Green Tea 500ml bottle, manufactured by Fraser & Neave Ltd Singapore. The NIP is a mandatory regulatory label under Singapore Food Regulations (Cap 283, Food Regulations). Values are per 100ml serving as declared on label: energy 17 kcal, carbohydrate 4.0g, sugar 4.0g, protein 0g, fat 0g, sodium 1mg. Vitamin C 6mg per 100ml is declared as an added antioxidant. This is classified as an approved branded/packaged food source equivalent to USDA Branded Food for Singapore-manufactured products.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-sweetened beverages and dilute sucrose solutions — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened tea and sucrose-containing beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Heaven and Earth Jasmine Green Tea in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at approximately 65 (medium) based on analogous entries for sucrose-sweetened dilute beverages and sweetened tea drinks at comparable sugar concentrations (4g sugar per 100ml). The University of Sydney GI Database lists sucrose at GI 65 (glucose reference = 100); a dilute sucrose-sweetened beverage at 4g/100ml is expected to have a GI in the range 60–70. Glycemic load per 500ml bottle = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 20) / 100 = 13.0. This is a medium GL. Estimation basis: analogous sucrose-sweetened beverage entries from the University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.guidance: Heaven and Earth Jasmine Green Tea contains negligible sodium (1mg/100ml, 5mg per bottle), posing no clinically meaningful blood pressure risk from sodium intake; however, the sugar content (20g/bottle) may contribute to weight gain and metabolic syndrome, which are secondary hypertension risk factors.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI 65 (medium), 20g free sugar per 500ml bottle, health halo misconception
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 500ml bottle delivers 20g of free sugar (approximately 5 teaspoons) with a glycemic load of 13 — a clinically significant sugar load for patients with Type 2 Diabetes. HEALTH HALO ALERT: Patients frequently perceive Heaven and Earth Green Tea as a healthy beverage due to the 'green tea' branding and association with EGCG antioxidant benefits; however, the polyphenol concentration in this RTD product is insufficient to confer the metabolic or anti-inflammatory benefits demonstrated in clinical studies using brewed green tea. The Singapore Food Agency Nutri-Grade C rating reflects this sugar burden. Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should be counselled that this product is a sugar-sweetened beverage and should be limited or avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 0g, trans fat 0g; free sugar 20g per bottle
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Heaven and Earth Jasmine Green Tea contains no saturated fat or trans fat, posing no direct atherogenic lipid risk; however, regular consumption of 20g free sugar per bottle may contribute to hypertriglyceridaemia and visceral adiposity, which are independent cardiovascular risk factors, and patients with cardiovascular disease should limit intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Heaven and Earth Jasmine Green Tea is a commercially produced ready-to-drink (RTD) sweetened jasmine-scented green tea beverage manufactured by Fraser & Neave (F&N) Ltd in Singapore. It is sold in a 500ml plastic bottle and is one of Singapore's most widely consumed packaged tea drinks, available in convenience stores (7-Eleven, Cheers), supermarkets, and hawker centres. The beverage is made from water, sugar, green tea extract, jasmine flavouring, and vitamin C (added as an antioxidant). Per 100ml, it provides 17 kcal, 4g carbohydrate (all sugar), 0g protein, 0g fat, and 1mg sodium. A full 500ml bottle delivers 85 kcal and 20g sugar — approximately 5 teaspoons of sugar. The product carries a Singapore Food Agency (SFA) Nutri-Grade C rating, reflecting its sugar content. Clinically, this product is the paradigmatic 'health halo' beverage in Singapore: patients with diabetes, obesity, or metabolic syndrome frequently select it as a 'healthy' alternative to carbonated soft drinks, incorrectly assuming that the green tea branding confers the antioxidant and metabolic benefits of brewed green tea. The EGCG and polyphenol concentration in this RTD product is far below the levels used in clinical studies demonstrating anti-inflammatory or glycaemic benefits. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium) with a glycemic load of 13 per bottle. Dietitians and clinicians in Singapore should specifically address this health halo misconception when counselling patients who consume this product.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the mandatory Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) on the Fraser & Neave Heaven and Earth Jasmine Green Tea 500ml bottle, which is a regulatory-compliant product label under Singapore Food Regulations. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sucrose-sweetened beverages at equivalent sugar concentration. Confidence is high for macronutrient values; GI is an approved analogous estimate.
notes: RTD beverage; protein_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a water-based sweetened tea with no protein-containing ingredients. Fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — contains no fat-containing ingredients. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are reported as -1 (not measured/not reported on NIP label) because the Singapore NIP for this product does not break down fat subtypes, which is consistent with the total fat being 0g. Water_g is reported as -1 (not measured) as water content is not declared on the NIP label; the beverage is predominantly water by volume but the precise water_g per 100g is not analytically confirmed in the source. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — no fibre-containing ingredients. Iron_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1 through b12, folate, vitamin_d, vitamin_e, zinc, selenium are all confirmed zero or not detected per NIP; these are not added to this product and are not naturally present in meaningful quantities in dilute sweetened green tea. Jasmine Tea (茉莉花茶, Mo Li Hua Cha) is the most famous scented tea in China, traditionally made from green tea leaves layered with fresh jasmine blossoms to absorb floral fragrance. The RTD commercial version uses jasmine flavouring rather than traditional scenting. The product received a Nutri-Grade C rating from the Singapore Food Agency (SFA) under the Healthier Choice Programme, reflecting its sugar content of 4g/100ml.
